name: What proof do I need that my tinnitus is from shop noise?
description: Audiograms, noise records, medical link to work.[1][2]

mainEntity.name: What proof do I need that my tinnitus is from shop noise?

acceptedAnswer.text: Audiograms, noise records, medical link to work.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description:
**Gather audiograms, workplace noise logs, employment records, and expert medical opinion linking loss to job.**

Proving causation requires showing exposure above 85 dB consistently without protection.[1][3] Coworker affidavits strengthen cases.

**Key factors:**
- Pre- and post-exposure hearing tests.[2]
- OSHA violation records or decibel measurements.
- Doctor's report ruling out non-work causes.
- Duration of employment in noisy role.

Preserve all docs; an attorney can subpoena employer records.[1] Don't delete personal notes.

Solid evidence overcomes denials.
